Nikolaus has coordinated the RFP process (shared RFP documents 2 June, requested alignment call 3 June, proposed workshop dates 5 June) and articulated functional requirements ('autonomous negotiation for orders below €10,000 must be included' on 21 May), demonstrating engagement and project ownership. However, there is no evidence of advocacy FOR Zycus (no introductions to CFO/VP/EB, no competitive positioning shared, no internal champion behavior such as pushing back on competitors or driving the agenda in Zycus's favor). On 5 June, Nikolaus stated 'Excellence lead is on vacation until 12 June' (referring to an unnamed internal lead) and 'Unix works closely with H&Z as potential project partner,' signaling that internal decision-makers and preferred partners exist but have not engaged with Zycus directly. Nikolaus's access to power is unknown: no CFO, VP Procurement (Erik Zwanikken), CTO/COO (Philipp Hahn), or CFO (Jan Villwock) has appeared on any call or been mentioned by Nikolaus as a sponsor. His role as external consultant (not internal Yunex employee per email domain @yunextraffic.com .ext) raises ghost risk if he loses influence or disengages before vendor selection (1-2 days away per Next Step 8 June). Current engagement: active as of 26 June (offer delivery) and 29 June (scheduled call per Next Step), but cooling risk exists if internal stakeholders (Excellence lead, IT Prague team, procurement VP) do not engage in the deep-dive workshop (never confirmed) or if Nikolaus's recommendation is overridden by an executive preference or competitor relationship (CRM MEDDPICC blocker: 'Yunex is currently working closely with H&Z. H&Z has an implementation spin off called aneon which is a premium impl. partner of Coupa'). The move is to multi-thread urgently: ask Nikolaus to introduce the Excellence lead, IT lead (Prague), and economic buyer (CFO or VP Procurement) into the conversation before vendor selection closes, and simultaneously develop an internal champion (someone with budget influence or access to the EB who will advocate for Zycus's AI differentiation over competitors).

identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain in manual P2O for indirect materials and services, specifically sub-€10k order negotiation inefficiency, confirmed by buyer but not yet quantified. Nikolaus Hoenig (external consultant, Evaluator) stated on 21 May 2026: 'autonomous negotiation for orders below €10,000 must be included in the P2P package,' identifying manual negotiation of low-value orders as a pain point Zycus's Anna agent must solve. CRM MEDDPICC (last updated 23 June) states 'Metrics: End-to-end P2O process coverage for Indirect Materials and Services,' and Next Step 8 June references the same scope. The 21 May call showcased Merlin Intake and Anna autonomous negotiation, with positive feedback: 'user-friendly interface and seamless handling of service versus goods procurement' and 'over 90% adoption without training' for Teams-based Merlin Intake. Nikolaus also asked 'Do we have references in Germany comparable in size with Yunex?' (21 May), signaling concern that the solution must fit their operational scale and regional context. The pain owner is not explicitly named on calls, but Nikolaus is the project consultant driving P2O evaluation, and the 5 June call referenced 'IT and procurement stakeholders' (unnamed) who will attend the deep-dive workshop, implying procurement operations owns the pain. However, no CFO, VP Procurement, or C-level has engaged to articulate strategic or financial pain (cost per order, manual touch-time, headcount burden), so the pain remains operationally confirmed but not elevated to executive sponsorship. The move is to quantify the pain with the buyer: ask Nikolaus (or the procurement/IT leads in the workshop) for current sub-€10k order volume, manual effort per order, error rate, and cost per transaction, then build a quantified ROI case anchored to Anna's automation and present it to the economic buyer before vendor selection closes.

decision_criteria.narrative: Confirmed across RFP documents and demo feedback, though not exhaustively detailed on calls. Yunex shared RFP documents for the P2O project on 2 June per Next Step, and the 5 June call referenced 'clustered functional and technical questions' in the RFP. On 21 May, buyer asked 'Do we have references in Germany comparable in size with Yunex?' and 'Does the platform support single sign-on?', signaling size/fit, regional reference credibility, and SSO as criteria. Platform confirmed to support SSO via preferred identity providers (21 May call notes). Two-way SAP S/4HANA integration via native plug-and-play connector was emphasized (21 May: 'Integration capabilities with the relevant stakeholder and S4HANA using plug-and-play connector supporting two-way data flow'), and Yunex confirmed they have S/4HANA (21 May call: 'Do you have S4HANA currently, or Yes?'). Multi-party approval workflow capability confirmed (21 May: 'Is it possible to have multiple parties to approve one purchase?'). Service procurement templating ('configure service templates based on best practices to avoid full redesign' per 21 May notes) and supplier onboarding ease ('robust supplier onboarding via simple email-link activation' per 21 May notes) also validated. The 5 June call emphasized 'two-hour deep dive after AFP response to ensure all technical and functional details are aligned before finalizing contracts,' indicating that final criteria sign-off is pending the workshop. Implementation partner flexibility (in-house Zycus, Big Four, or regional consultancy) was discussed 5 June, with Nikolaus stating 'preference to finalize software selection first; implementation partner discussion deferred to second phase,' so partner capability is a criterion but evaluated separately. The move is to confirm the weighted scoring model and any unstated criteria (e.g., Merlin AI weight, reference customer requirements, pricing cap) in the deep-dive workshop or via Nikolaus before RFP scoring closes.
